items.title: DSpark: Confidence-Scheduled Speculative Decoding with Semi-Autoregressive Generation
items.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates Large Language Model (LLM) inference by decoupling draft generation from target verification. While recent parallel drafters efficiently propose long token sequences in a single forward pass, they suffer from rapid acceptance decay due to a lack of inter-token dependencies. Furthermore, indiscriminately verifying these extended blocks wastes critical batch capacity on tokens with high rejection risks, severely degrading throughput in high-concurrency serving systems. We introduce DSpark, a speculative decoding framework that unifies high-throughput parallel generation with adaptive, load-aware verification. To maintain draft quality, DSpark utilizes a semi-autoregressive architecture, coupling a parallel backbone with a lightweight sequential module, to introduce intra-block dependency modeling and mitigate suffix decay. To optimize system efficiency, DSpark employs confidence-scheduled verification, dynamically tailoring the verification length for each request based on estimated prefix survival probabilities and engine-specific throughput profiles. On offline benchmarks across diverse domains, DSpark substantially improves the accepted length over state-of-the-art autoregressive and parallel drafters. When deployed within the DeepSeek-V4 serving system under live user traffic, DSpark successfully mitigates verification waste. Compared to the established production baseline (MTP-1), DSpark accelerates per-user generation speeds by 60 to 85 percent at matched throughput levels. More importantly, by preventing severe throughput degradation under strict interactivity constraints, it enables performance tiers that were previously unattainable, shifting the Pareto frontier of our serving system.
items.llm_evidence_en: speculative decoding for LLM inference acceleration
items.llm_evidence_cn: 投机解码加速大模型推理
items.llm_evidence: 投机解码加速大模型推理
items.canonical_evidence: 投机解码加速大模型推理
items.llm_tldr_en: DSpark proposes a speculative decoding framework with semi-autoregressive draft generation and adaptive verification to improve LLM throughput, addressing inefficiencies in parallel draft acceptance and batch usage.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型推理中，投机解码通过解耦草稿生成和目标验证来加速，但现有并行草稿方法因缺乏令牌间依赖导致接受率低，且盲目验证浪费批处理容量。DSpark 提出半自回归架构生成高质量草稿，并采用自适应负载感知验证策略，在保持吞吐量的同时提高令牌接受率。实验表明，DSpark 在高并发场景下显著提升推理速度。
items.llm_tldr: 大语言模型推理中，投机解码通过解耦草稿生成和目标验证来加速，但现有并行草稿方法因缺乏令牌间依赖导致接受率低，且盲目验证浪费批处理容量。DSpark 提出半自回归架构生成高质量草稿，并采用自适应负载感知验证策略，在保持吞吐量的同时提高令牌接受率。实验表明，DSpark 在高并发场景下显著提升推理速度。

items.title: Optimizing ML Workload Partitioning between CPUs and CIM Accelerators for Heterogeneous Computing
items.abstract: Computing-in-Memory (CIM) accelerators execute Matrix-Vector Multiplications (MVMs) in memory, making them a compelling solution for Machine Learning (ML) workloads. However, existing ML workload partitioning approaches for CIM accelerators do not fully account for Resistive Random Access Memory (RRAM) constraints such as limited memory, high write latency, and limited endurance. They also neglect parallelism, low-level architectural effects, or the Central Processing Unit (CPU) as a complementary compute resource. To address these limitations, we propose an Integer Linear Programming (ILP)-based workload partitioning framework for heterogeneous CPU-CIM systems. It minimizes end-to-end inference latency under RRAM constraints, captures parallelism, and combines empirical profiling with analytical models. Using our framework, heterogeneous CPU-CIM execution achieves speedups of up to 30.9x over CPU-only execution on an edge CPU and 7.3x over a high-performance CPU. A Design Space Exploration (DSE) yields further design insights for future CIM accelerators.
items.llm_evidence_en: heterogeneous computing for ML workload partitioning

items.llm_tldr_en: An ILP-based framework partitions ML workloads between CPUs and CIM accelerators for heterogeneous computing, minimizing latency under RRAM constraints.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 异构计算中，CPU与CIM加速器的协同面临内存限制和访存长尾问题。现有划分方法忽略RRAM约束及CPU作为补充资源。该工作提出整数线性规划框架，在RRAM限制下最小化推理延迟，同时考虑并行性和底层架构效应。实验验证相比基线显著降低延迟。

items.title: Adaptive Inference Batching using Policy Gradients
items.abstract: Inference serving systems must balance throughput and latency under bursty, heterogeneous workloads, yet the industry standard remains static batching policies that require manual tuning and cannot adapt to shifting traffic. We investigate whether reinforcement learning (RL) can learn adaptive batching and routing policies that outperform these heuristics, training REINFORCE and PPO agents on a discrete-event simulator validated against queuing theory and production traces (Azure Functions, BurstGPT). We formulate the problem as an MDP over queue state, request type and GPU availability, evaluating across standard Poisson traffic, extreme bursts, real-world traces and heterogeneous multi-GPU routing.   Our central finding is a clear boundary condition for RL's value in systems problems. In single-GPU settings, a well-tuned static batching policy is already near-optimal under Poisson-like arrivals and RL offers only marginal gains (+0.1% to +1.0%). In multi-GPU heterogeneous routing, however, where fast and slow requests compete for shared resources, the agent discovers a workload-segregation policy that eliminates Head-of-Line blocking, yielding a 3.5x (348%) improvement over Round-Robin and a 48% improvement over the strongest heuristic baseline (Shortest-Queue), with 60% higher throughput and 25% lower latency while respecting SLA constraints. The policy generalizes to unseen bursty and real-world traffic despite training only on synthetic Poisson arrivals and an attention-augmented policy network converges roughly 20% faster than an MLP baseline.   These results suggest RL's advantage over engineered heuristics concentrates in combinatorial, multi-resource decisions rather than single-resource temporal scheduling, a practical distinction for deciding where learned policies justify their engineering cost in production inference infrastructure.
items.llm_evidence_en: adaptive batching policy using RL for inference serving under heterogeneous workloads
items.llm_evidence_cn: 使用强化学习动态调整批处理策略以适应异构推理负载
items.llm_evidence: 使用强化学习动态调整批处理策略以适应异构推理负载
items.canonical_evidence: 使用强化学习动态调整批处理策略以适应异构推理负载
items.llm_tldr_en: Investigates RL-based adaptive batching and routing for inference serving, using REINFORCE and PPO on a discrete-event simulator, finding boundary conditions where RL outperforms static policies.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 推理服务系统在突发异构负载下需要动态调优。本文研究强化学习（REINFORCE和PPO）学习自适应批处理和路由策略。基于离散事件模拟器验证，评估了标准泊松流量、极端突发和真实跟踪。主要发现是RL在特定边界条件下明显优于静态策略，提供了一种自动调优方案。
items.llm_tldr: 推理服务系统在突发异构负载下需要动态调优。本文研究强化学习（REINFORCE和PPO）学习自适应批处理和路由策略。基于离散事件模拟器验证，评估了标准泊松流量、极端突发和真实跟踪。主要发现是RL在特定边界条件下明显优于静态策略，提供了一种自动调优方案。
